content: Members and Officers shall maintain their personal appearance that displays the discipline and professional conduct expected in the Boys' Brigade.
No form of body ornaments or accessories (earrings, nose rings, rings other than wedding bands, bracelets, anklets, toe rings) are to be worn in uniform.

topic: Personal Appearance - Ear Sticks for Girls
content: Clear plastic ear sticks or studs may be used by girls only to keep piercings open while in uniform.
Nails (fingers and toes) must be kept short and clean. No nail accessories (polish, color, coating) are allowed on Parade.
Hairstyles must be clean, conservative, practical, and must not prevent proper wearing of Brigade Headgear (Field Service Cap or Glengarry).
Members shall wear their hair in accordance with their school regulations (e.g. SJKC). If the school offers variety, Members shall adhere to these regulations.

topic: Personal Appearance - Hair Dyeing Prohibited for Members
content: Members are prohibited from dyeing their hair.

topic: Personal Appearance - Hair Dye for Officers
content: Officers: No hair dye of striking colours is allowed. Only natural shades similar to their ethnicity are permitted. Officers shall lead by example.
Wigs, extensions, cornrows, and dreadlocks are strictly prohibited. Skinhead is prohibited unless for cranial medical treatment or religious requirements.

Members and Officers may acquire special exceptions from their Company Captain for certain seasons, performances, or religious adherence (e.g. long nails for instruments, long hair for ballet recital).
Hair must be kept clean and neat.
No hair should lie on the forehead.
Hair at the back must not touch the collar.
Sideburns must be clean shaven and no longer than beyond the tragus (ear hole).
No facial hair is allowed unless growing out for a performance or religious adherence.

topic: Hair - Male Officers - Clean and Neat
content: Hair must be kept clean and neat.
No hair should hang in front of the forehead.
Hair at the back must not touch the collar.
Sideburns must be clean-shaven and no longer than beyond the tragus (ear hole).
No beard is allowed unless growing out for a performance or religious adherence.
Moustaches are permitted if kept short, trimmed, above and not wider than the upper lip. No goatee or soul patch.
Hair must be kept clean and neat.
No hair should hang in front of the forehead.
Hair at the back shall be tied into a single ponytail or braid so it does not hang freely around the neck and shoulders.
Sideburns shall be no longer than 5cm from the earlobe.
Girls shall use black hairpins and clips only for keeping hair neat and tidy, not for decoration.
No hair should lie on the forehead.
Female Officer's hair at the back shall be tied into a single ponytail or braid so it does not hang freely around the neck and shoulders.
Sideburns shall be no longer than 5cm from the earlobe.
Female Officers shall use black hairpins and clips only for keeping hair neat and tidy, not for decoration.

The blazer is worn over the Officer's Shirt or a plain white long-sleeved shirt with sleeves down and fully buttoned. Collar badges are fastened to the upper lapel and worn perpendicularly. All buttons are navy blue.

topic: Officer - Ceremonial Dress - Name Tag Placement
content: The name tag is worn above the right breast pocket on the blazer.
The design of a female Officer's blazer is similar but fitted for a lady.
Male Officers wear trousers similar to those worn with Day Dress.
Female Officers wear a Ceremonial Skirt. It is plain-design, knee-length, navy blue, 'A' line cut, with two side pockets and a maximum of two stitched cuts. Strictly for Ceremonial Dress only.
White with Military Cutting. White buttons. Two epaulettes. Selvage strip. Two breast pockets with buttoned flaps. No pleats on back. Design identical for men and women.
The collar width shall be 2 inches.
The selvage strip width is 1 1/4 inches. All buttons are white.
All epaulette buttons are white. Epaulette length varies according to shoulder breadth.
The shoulder flash with 'BRIGED PUTERA MALAYSIA' is sewn on both sleeves, 3/4' from the epaulette and centred with it.
The Officer's shirt may be short-sleeved (just above elbows) or long-sleeved.
For Formal Dress, Officers wear the Officer's Shirt with necktie and tie clip.
For Day Dress, Officers wear the Officer's Shirt with collar unbuttoned and no tie. If long-sleeved, sleeves must be rolled up above the elbow with a 2-inch fold.
If not wearing the Officer's shirt, only plain white long-sleeved shirts may be worn with Ceremonial Dress.
The Brigade T-Shirt designed by National HQ is the default for Drill Kit and PT Kit.
Company, District, Region, State, or Event T-Shirts may be worn in place of the Brigade T-Shirt for Drill Kit or PT Kit, but only during the event or within the Council's jurisdiction.
The Glengarry is worn by Officers and Warrant Officers only.
Tilted slightly right, approximately one inch above the right ear and two fingers' breadth above the right eyebrow.
The Glengarry Badge is worn on top of the Rosette on the left side. Hair must be concealed from the forehead.
White letters on a black plastic background, 75mm x 25mm.
Full Name (THAM CHWEN FONG) or first name only if full name >15 characters (RAVINDRAN instead of RAVINDRAN A/L ARUMUGAM). Christian/first name precedes initials and surname.
Worn just above the right breast pocket for Day and Formal Dress. On the right breast above pocket level for Ceremonial Dress. On the right breast area of the T-shirt for Drill Kit and PT Kit.
BBHQ Staff: Black letters (name and position) with BB Logo on a silver background layered with epoxy, 75mm x 25mm.
Worn over the left shoulder with loop under the left epaulette. End fastened to the side of the left breast pocket.
Should not dangle more than 1' breadth below the breast pocket.
A standard whistle (straight for 9-figure) is fastened to the end.
Plain navy blue with no design.
Worn only with Ceremonial or Formal Dress.
The tip of the tie must not cover the belt buckle.
A standard BB Tie Clip must be fastened to the tie.
Plain navy blue with two side pockets and one back pocket with no flap. Straight cut without pleats.
Same design as male but fitted for a lady. May be worn for Day and Formal Dress only. Female Officers wear Ceremonial Skirt for Ceremonial Dress.
Black nylon canvas snake grip belt with aluminium/steel buckle embossed with the BB Logo.
Navy blue socks of plain design.
Plain black leather shoes or boots with laces.
Plain black court shoes with maximum 2' heels.
Worn below the top flap of the left breast pocket on the Blazer.
Only the highest Brigade Service Medal achieved by the Officer shall be worn.
Highest officer medal on the rightmost side, other non-BB medals in descending order towards the left. If only one medal, fastened squarely at the centre of the left breast pocket.
Worn just above the left breast pocket for Day Dress or Formal Dress.

topic: Officer Rank - Lanyard Colour - Dark Blue
content: Dark Blue: Warrant Officers, BBHQ Officer, BBHQ Assistant Officer.
Green: Teacher Advisor.
Black: Lieutenant, BBHQ Assistant Executive Secretary, BBHQ Senior Executive, BBHQ Executive.
Red: Captain, BBHQ Executive Secretary.
Orange: Honorary Captain.
Purple: Chaplain.

topic: Officer Rank - Glengarry Rosette Colour - Black
content: Black Rosette: Warrant Officer, Teacher Advisor, Lieutenant, BBHQ Assistant Executive Secretary, BBHQ Senior Executive, BBHQ Executive.

topic: Officer Rank - Glengarry Rosette Colour - Red
content: Red Rosette: Captain, BBHQ Executive Secretary.

topic: Officer Rank - Glengarry Rosette Colour - Orange
content: Orange Rosette: Honorary Captain.

topic: Officer Rank - Glengarry Rosette Colour - Purple
content: Purple Rosette: Chaplain.
The OTP Badge can be worn on Day Dress, Formal Dress, and Ceremonial Uniform.
Worn above the name tag with no gap between the OTP badge and the name tag.

topic: Officer - OTP Badge - Highest Level Only
content: Only the highest level achieved OTP Badge shall be worn at any given time.
Officers' Glengarry with Warrant Officers' cap badge. White Officer's shirt (short or long). Warrant Officers' collar badges. Name tag (white on black). Green Lanyard.
Plain navy-blue long trousers with two side pockets and one back pocket with no flap. Straight cut without pleats.

topic: Teacher Advisor Uniform - Belt and Tie
content: Officers' Belt. Dark blue tie and tie-clip for Formal Dress only.
Men: Navy or Dark Blue socks, plain black leather shoes or boots with laces. Women: Plain black court shoes with max 2' heels.

topic: Teacher Advisor Uniform - No Ceremonial or Drill Kit
content: There is no Ceremonial Dress or Drill Kit for Teacher Advisor.

topic: Primer Uniform - Shirt and Dress Codes
content: Long-sleeved Officer's shirt. Ceremonial: with necktie and tie clip, sleeves down. Day: sleeves rolled above elbow, 2-inch fold.

Field Service Cap, Senior Section Cap Badge, Primer's Epaulette, Senior's Name Tag, Officer's Necktie and Tie Clip, Officer's Trousers, Officer's Belt, Officer's Footwear, Senior Section Brigade T-Shirt.
PT Kit: Training Shorts/Track Pants and Sports shoes.
Awards are worn only in Ceremonial Dress or Day Dress. Not worn in Drill Kit or PT Kit.
Worn on the left side, above the name tag.
Worn on the left arm, centred below the shoulder flash.
Primer wears only either the Founder's Award or President Award (whichever higher attained in Senior Section).
If a Primer with Founder's/President's Badge also gains the Robert Davis Award, both are worn on equal level below the shoulder flash, with Robert Davis on the left.
Navy Blue with Military Cutting. Navy Blue buttons. Selvage strip. Two breast pockets with buttoned flaps. Two epaulettes. No pleats on back. Design identical for Boys and Girls.
Collar width: 2 inches. Selvage strip: 1 1/4 inches. All buttons are navy blue.
All epaulette buttons are navy blue. Length varies according to shoulder breadth.
The shoulder flash with 'BRIGED PUTERA MALAYSIA' is sewn on both sleeves, 3/4' from the epaulette and centred with it.
Ceremonial: long-sleeved with sky blue tie tucked under the haversack. Sleeves down and fully buttoned.
Day: long-sleeved with sleeves rolled up above the elbow. The folding shall be 2 inches in width.
The Brigade T-Shirt from BB Shop is the default for Drill Kit and PT Kit.
Company, District, Region, State, or Event T-Shirts may be worn in place for Drill Kit or PT Kit during the event or within Council jurisdiction.
Tilted slightly right, two fingers from right ear, two fingers from right eyebrow. Hair concealed from forehead.
The circular background of 'BB' is Blue.
Worn on the left side, just above the breast pocket.
Male: black letters on white plastic background, 70mm x 20mm, with Member's name and Company name.
Female: white letters on sky-blue plastic background, 70mm x 20mm, with Member's name and Company name.
Full Name or first name only if >15 characters. Christian/first name precedes initials and surname.

topic: Senior NCO Lanyard - Colour and Who Wears
content: White lanyard worn by NCOs, Sergeants and Staff Sergeants in Ceremonial Dress or Day Dress.
Over left shoulder, loop under left epaulette, fastened to left breast pocket side. Not more than 1' below pocket.
Standard whistle (straight for 9-figure) fastened to the end.

topic: Senior NCO Field Service Rank - Wearing
content: NCOs, Sergeants and Staff Sergeants Field Service Rank should wear on their RIGHT Hand, with ranks logo pointed outward. It should only be worn in Drill Kit or PT Kit

topic: Staff Sergeant Sash - Colour and How to Wear
content: Staff Sergeant Sash is blue. It is worn tightly over the left shoulder under the left epaulette, on top of the haversack and passing under the belt on the right side.

topic: Sergeant Sash - Colour and How to Wear
content: Sergeant Sash is red. It is worn tightly over the left shoulder under the left epaulette, on top of the haversack and passing under the belt on the right side.
Senior's Shirt, blue sash over the haversack, chevron with badges, and parade cane (optional).
Navy Blue Shirt, blue sash over the haversack, chevron with badges and parade cane (optional).
Senior's Shirt, red sash over the haversack, chevron with badges and parade cane (optional).
Navy Blue Shirt, red sash over the haversack and chevron with badges. No parade cane.
Sky blue plain design.
Worn only in Ceremonial Dress, between the shirt and haversack. No BB Tie Clip.
The tip of the tie must not cover the belt buckle.
Navy blue with two side pockets and one back pocket with no flap. Straight cut without pleats.
Same as boys' but may have a maximum of 2 stitched cuts (non-pleated) in the front and back to suit the shape.
Brown or black leather belt with 2 runners and a brass buckle embossed with BB logo.
Worn tightly around the waist with buckle in the centre and the two runners placed as far apart as possible.
White haversack worn tightly over the right shoulder under the right epaulette, passing under the belt on the left side. Pouch rests on the left hip.
A brass button is fastened to the pouch.
Senior members wear navy blue socks of plain design with their uniform.
Plain black leather shoes or boots with laces. Same for Boys and Girls.
Metal parts: Loop, Slide, Brass Button, Split Hook.

To assemble the Senior haversack: Step 1: Insert the longer strap into the slide and pull the slide to the centre of the strap. Step 2: Insert the remaining edge of the strap into the loop. If not too long, secure the Velcro around the loop and at the slide. Step 3: Insert the Velcro edge of the strap around the centre bar of the slide and secure. Step 4: Fasten the brass button and split hook on the pouch. Then adjust the haversack by pulling the slide up and down.
Adjust the haversack by pulling the slide up and down.
The Target Badge is a Senior section award worn on the RIGHT arm.
Target Badge goes on the top row first, followed by Proficiency badges in alphabetical order (5 per row). If the final row has less than 5 badges, they are centred.

topic: Senior Awards - Right Arm - Armlet vs Chevron
content: Proficiency badges and Target badge worn on an armlet. NCOs wear on their Chevrons.

topic: Senior Awards - Left Arm - Founder's Badge
content: Founder's Badge is worn on the LEFT arm, centred below the Shoulder Flash.

topic: Senior Awards - Left Arm - Second Row
content: Second row: President's Badge (left), Duke of Edinburgh Award (centre), Junior Gold Award (right).

topic: Senior Awards - Left Arm - Scholastic Badges
content: Scholastic Badges in a row below President's Badge: Gold Scholastics Badge on the leftmost, descending order.

topic: Senior Awards - Left Arm - NCO and Service Badges
content: NCO Proficiency Star in the middle, Long Years' Service (left) and Three-Year Service (right) flanking it.

topic: Senior Awards - Left Arm - Service Badges
content: Junior Service Badge on the leftmost of the row below, with One Year Service badges in a row continuously.
The Link Badge, awarded to Juniors who passed the prescribed test and transferred to Senior Section, is worn just above the name tag.
Available for Companies required to comply with school, church, or institution dress codes. Applied to ALL Senior Girls in the Company.
Plain-design, knee-length, navy blue, 'A' line cut, two side pockets, maximum 2 stitched cuts, fitted from natural waistline.
Belt loops: 2 1/2 inch tall, 1/2 inch wide to accommodate the Senior's Belt.
Girls shall wear black 1/4 length tights or black 'safety shorts' underneath.
Worn on natural waistline. All tops tucked in, displaying waistband and belt loops. Senior Belt worn (polished and clean).
Plain calf-length navy blue socks and plain black leather boots/shoes. Socks visible ~1 inch above boot collar and pull loop.
Navy Blue with Military Cutting. Navy Blue buttons. Selvage strip. Two breast pockets with buttoned flaps. Two epaulettes. No pleats on back. Design identical for Boys and Girls.
Collar width: 2 inches. Selvage strip: 1 1/4 inches. All buttons are navy blue.
All epaulette buttons are navy blue. Length varies according to shoulder breadth.
No long-sleeves for Junior's Shirt.
The shoulder flash with 'BRIGED PUTERA MALAYSIA' is sewn on both sleeves, 3/4'' from the epaulette and centred with it.
The Brigade T-Shirt from BB Shop is the default for Drill Kit and PT Kit.
May be worn as an alternative uniform for platoons meeting after school or Companies in interior communities.
Company, District, Region, State, or Event T-Shirts may be worn in place for Drill Kit or PT Kit during the event or within Council jurisdiction.
Tilted slightly right, two fingers from right ear, two fingers from right eyebrow. Hair concealed from forehead.
The circular background of 'BB' is Red.
Same as Senior Section.
Navy-blue wristband with BB Logo worn by the Chief, Leading, and Assistant Leading Boy/Girl.
The Junior wristband is worn around the right hand, with logos pointed outward.
Chief Leading Boy/Girl wear a RED single rope lanyard in Day Dress.
Assistant Leading Boy/Girl wear a BLUE single rope lanyard in Day Dress.
Leading Boy/Girl wear a WHITE single rope lanyardn Day Dress.
Over left shoulder, loop under left epaulette, fastened to left breast pocket side. Not more than 1' below pocket.
Standard whistle (straight or 9-figure) fastened to the end.
One-inch white canvas belt with aluminium/steel buckle embossed with BB logo.
Navy blue shorts with two side pockets and one back pocket.
Knee-length navy blue socks of plain design.
Plain black canvas shoes with laces. Same for Boys and Girls.
Junior Recruitment badge and One-year service are pinned on a Junior Chevron, sewn on the LEFT arm.
Junior Section Achievement Awards are pinned on a Junior Chevron, sewn on the RIGHT arm.
The Brigade T-Shirt from BB Shop is the default for Pre-Junior's uniform.
Company, District, Region, State, or Event T-Shirts may be worn in place during the event or within Council jurisdiction.
Tilted slightly right, one finger from right ear, two fingers from right eyebrow. Hair concealed from forehead.
Same as Junior Section. Circular background of 'BB' is Red.
Same as Senior Section.
One-inch white canvas belt with aluminium/steel buckle embossed with BB logo.
Navy blue shorts with two side pockets and one back pocket.
Knee-length navy blue socks of plain design.
Plain black canvas shoes with laces or Velcro. Same for Boys and Girls.
Ironed and sewed on the right sleeve of the T-Shirt.
Placed in the order shown in the diagram.
